rationale: Compliance axis activation is non-negotiable when SOC 2 and traceability are explicitly in scope
Must distinguish multi-customer platform from multi-tenant architecture (false-positive restraint)
Operability scoring must reflect team experience reality, not theoretical pattern fit
Blocking rule enforcement when team capacity makes a candidate operationally fatal
Anti-monolith bias restraint — genuine strengths must be acknowledged before spec-grounded rejection
Confidence labels must use documented evidence instead of defaulting to assumed
Adaptability tiebreaker applies only within the same fit band per constitution
Tradeoff tension must name a genuine sacrifice, not a fake tension
Brownfield extraction requires shadow, reconciliation, rollback, split-brain prevention, cutover criteria
Conditional skill loading discipline — no irrelevant AI skills, yes data-engineering
Contradicting executive constraints must be reframed and escalated, not papered over
Near-term compliance roadmap must produce approve-with-conditions, not ignore or block
Documented dependency unreliability must lower scores or mandate durable buffering
Bounded pattern application is valid — all-or-nothing event sourcing framing is wrong
Migration-phase operational burden must lower transition-period operability scores
Measured evidence on the old schema must not inflate confidence for the new architecture
Explicit shadow-mode constraint is non-negotiable — no abbreviated cutover
Financial rollback means compensating entries, not destructive code revert
Stated hot/cold retention boundary must drive tiered storage design
GIL trap restraint — I/O-bound Python at stated load must not trigger a language change
Financial strangler-fig migration should be data-consistency-led, not HTTP-proxy-only
Dependency-assumption restraint — Temporal must not be penalized for unrelated Kafka risk
Connection exhaustion at stated throughput must surface as an architectural condition
No clean single winner exists — unresolved gaps or bounded hybrid must be documented
Over-blocking restraint — bounded event sourcing must not be hard-blocked on team gap alone
Impossible literal constraint must be reframed to practical durability with approval path
Dual-write without automated reconciliation is fatal — under-blocking trap tightened post-canary
Single write authority must be unambiguous at every migration phase — split-brain trap tightened post-canary
Customer segmentation data must drive cohort rollout, not all-at-once migration
Actor identity must propagate through every migration-introduced boundary for audit integrity
HIPAA readiness requires infrastructure-level validation, not code-level controls alone
Documented trigger-bypass evidence may justify a constitution Simplicity Gate exception
Current state must be evaluated as a formal baseline to justify migration ROI
